THEME (Moti's call): a BREAK from the rec-cam/face-rec arc to fix bugs + add a feature. Champ & Chimp, warm short session, both wins verified LIVE. 

FIX 1 - WhatsApp stuck 401 (commit 99a3427 'fix(whatsapp): auto-purge dead session on 401 + hard-clean button'). The recurring 'login cert stuck on disk' bug: red banner `Connection Failure (401) {reason:401,location:lla}`, could never reconnect. ROOT CAUSE: WhatsApp invalidates the linked device -> Baileys closes with statusCode 401 (= DisconnectReason.loggedOut, VERIFIED in node_modules/baileys/lib/Types: connectionClosed=428, connectionLost/timedOut=408, loggedOut=401, badSession=500, restartRequired=515, forbidden=403, connectionReplaced=440, multideviceMismatch=411). The code correctly did NOT auto-reconnect on 401, BUT never deleted the dead creds in .baileys-auth/test-whatsapp/ -> every 'התחבר' reloaded the same dead creds via useMultiFileAuthState -> instant 401 again, NO QR ever. The only cleanup (logout() -> fs.rm) was UI-UNREACHABLE because the logout button only renders when connected===true (after 401 you're 'closed'). FIXES in src/lib/whatsapp-test.ts + src/app/test/whatsapp/WhatsAppTestPage.tsx: (a) AUTO-PURGE on dead-creds codes (401/403/500) in the connection.update close handler -> wipes auth dir + drops to 'idle' (keeps lastError so UI explains why) -> next connect = fresh QR. (b) 'ניקוי מלא' (hard-clean) button -> shown whenever NOT connected (header) + inside the login modal under any error; POSTs the existing /api/test/whatsapp/logout. (c) LOGOUT-HANG fix (the old carry-over): socket.logout() does a network round-trip that hangs forever on a dead socket -> wrapped in withTimeout(...,4000). Extracted purgeAuth()/resetRuntime()/isDeadCredsCode() helpers. DEPLOY: wiped the live stuck dir (creds.json from Jun 7), next build + pm2 restart raam-manage; Moti re-scanned the QR -> reconnected live (JID 972509844013, state connected). VERIFIED end-to-end. 

FEATURE 2 - Cameras wall page (commit 99b9f6d 'feat(cameras): all-cameras wall page'). New /cameras page ('מצלמות') = all 6 building cameras (the CAMERAS registry in src/lib/gates.ts: street .60, upper .107, ramp .61, road=cam29 on .137 DVR, lower .112, lobby .119) as a LIVE grid that fills the screen EVENLY (auto-rows-fr + flex-1 -> 3x2 on the lobby PC, 2-col fallback; object-cover = even 'squares'). All lobby staff (not manager-only). Sidebar link added (Cctv icon, under אירועים). KEY REFACTOR (Moti wanted each tile to BE the same window as the gate popup, WITH the expand + motion-detection icons): extracted the shared camera panel into NEW src/components/camera/CameraFeed.tsx (snapshot polling + detection MJPEG + the control icons). GateLiveView (only consumer = GateControl) became a THIN popup wrapper around CameraFeed (kept its 12s auto-close, paused on fullscreen/detect via an onDetectChange callback -> the stable setDetect setter; behavior-preserving). NEW CameraTile = the inline grid version (own useFullscreen ref, no auto-close, no X). ONE source of truth -> both windows share the detection-toggle + fullscreen and stay in sync as features grow. CameraFeed PAUSES snapshot polling when the tab is hidden (visibilitychange); wall refreshes at 1s vs the popup's 400ms (refreshMs prop) to spare the cameras; 'fill' prop stretches the body (flex-1) vs fixed aspect-video. Session 28 (2026-06-10, past midnight). Workplace/prod machine, semantix NOT installed (native tools + file memory, same as 12-27). Start HEAD 84a027a, final HEAD 94ef525, 3 commits, all pushed, tree clean. THEME: refactor-driven — modularized the two vision services AND finally killed the face-rec SPEED blocker. Champ & Chimp, warm session. 

COMMITS: (1) c537f79 refactor(face): split the 1017-line vision/face_probe.py monolith into a vision/face/ package — 14 single-responsibility modules (settings/audio/door/quality/gallery/engine/accumulator/enroll/overlay/events/worker/service/server/cli + __init__). THE decoupling wins: a mutable Settings singleton replaced ~15 module globals (the /threshold handler now mutates one object, no more `global X` soup); CameraWorker orchestrates via INJECTED collaborators (engine/gallery/door/events) instead of reaching for globals; engine.py = the isolated model-swap SEAM; gallery (templates) decoupled from engine (model). face_probe.py stays a 3-line shim so pm2 (cwd=vision, face_probe.py --cam lobby --port 8090) is unchanged. Behavior-preserving, verified live (loaded s2/s3, knobs at session-27 baseline, /selftest 0.65fps). 

(2) 589abae feat(face): swap RECOGNITION buffalo_l (ArcFace ResNet50 w600k_r50, ~1475ms/frame = 0.65fps live) -> MobileFaceNet (buffalo_s w600k_mbf), KEEPING buffalo_l SCRFD for DETECTION (its reach finds the far 30-65px fisheye faces = what enables an early/distance open). det_pack/rec_pack are now settings. LOAD TRAP (crash-looped 154x first): FaceAnalysis ASSERTS a detector — you can't build it allowed_modules=['recognition'] only; fix = load the ArcFace ONNX directly via insightface.model_zoo.get_model from the pack's w600k_*.onnx (glob, cold-cache FaceAnalysis-download fallback). MEASURED ~183ms/frame on a real single-face lobby frame = ~5.5fps, 8X FASTER. (t1 /selftest understates at 573ms — it's a CROWD image so detection-bound; real single-face is faster.) Re-enrolled (embeddings are model-specific; forgot the r50 s2/s3): Moti enrolled via the APP UI as label s1 (9 samples, head-turns) so the app name-map stays in sync. 

(3) 94ef525 refactor(vision): split vision/server.py (433 lines, the raam-vision YOLO service) -> vision/detection/ package, 7 modules mirroring face/ (settings/detections/worker/service/server/cli + __init__). server.py stays a thin shim (pm2 unchanged). Verified live: /healthz, /detections/lobby cold-start (2 potted plants w/ track-ids), /snapshot 565KB, lazy worker spins up on demand; endpoints byte-identical so the app's scan-eye toggle works with ZERO app changes. NOTE name distinction: detect.py (CLI viewer + CAMERAS registry) vs detection/ (the service package) — no clash. 

THE BIG LESSON (Moti's instinct, PROVEN via git): the face-rec 'got slow/late' was TWO independent things we'd conflated — (a) we OVER-GATED on pixel distance in the s25-27 precision push (px_full=60 ramp zeroed-out the far/small/head-down faces that USED to open it), and (b) the model was genuinely slow. Session 24 (2026-06-07 into 2026-06-08, ran past midnight) — ONE OF THE BIG ONES. Workplace/prod machine, semantix NOT installed (native tools + file memory). Start HEAD 10c896e. TWO arcs landed: (A) FIXED the vision detection lag + efficiency, (B) BUILT FACE RECOGNITION END-TO-END and made the building OPEN ITS OWN LOBBY DOOR for Moti's face — 'like a sci-fi spaceship'. 

ARC A — vision perf (COMMITTED d649782, pushed): the YOLO detection feed drifted further behind the live cam the longer it ran (5-10s by end of day, 'barely moving') — root cause was the worker reading RTSP frames in FIFO order on ONE thread, so when CPU YOLO (~12fps) couldn't keep the camera's 25fps the ffmpeg backlog grew unbounded. Fix in vision/server.py = split CameraWorker into a GRABBER thread (drains the camera, keeps ONLY the newest frame) + an INFERENCE thread (always runs on newest, drops stale). Latency now bounded to ~1 frame forever; verified ~99% in sync. Plus: imgsz 640->480 (~1.6x), torch threads -> 6 (MEASURED sweet spot for yolo11n on this box: ~36fps vs only ~18fps at 12 threads — past ~6 the thread-sync overhead beats parallelism!), cv2 threads 2, MAX_INFER_FPS cap 20, idle-stop 8s->3s, Connection:close on one-shot endpoints (no keep-alive thread pileup). Fixed a /healthz crash from the refactor (stale .thread attr). KEY MEASUREMENT GOTCHA that burned ~an hour: polling /detections only at the start+end of a window lets the lazy worker idle out mid-window -> fake low fps (1.5/6.6); must poll CONTINUOUSLY to measure real fps. Box sits ~80% idle. 

ARC B — FACE RECOGNITION (the headline; lives in vision/, face_probe.py committed, faces_db.json gitignored as biometric data): installed insightface 1.0.1 + onnxruntime 1.26.0 (CPU) in the vision venv; model = buffalo_l (SCRFD detect + ArcFace w600k recognition), auto-downloaded ~280MB. Built vision/face_probe.py — a standalone face rec test rig serving annotated MJPEG on its OWN port 8090 (LAN-bound 0.0.0.0 so Moti watches on a LAPTOP on the building wifi at http://10.0.0.127:8090/ — the laptop-as-feedback-screen was the unlock for Moti's constraint that he can't watch the PC while standing at the door). Pipeline: grabber(newest frame) -> SCRFD detect biggest face -> ArcFace embedding -> cosine vs enrolled template; banner shows MATCH/UNKNOWN + score + face px, color-coded. THE WINS, all verified LIVE with Moti at the .119 lobby cam (top-right fisheye, dim 1am): (1) detection+pixel-size measuring worked; this wide ceiling cam yields ~yellow 70-100px at natural spots (green only very close) — fine for cooperative rec; a proper face-height pool cam later gives 150-250px easily. (5) TUNED LIVE to the sweet spot: threshold 0.30, px 30 (lowered via the live endpoint while Moti read the on-screen face-px numbers from the laptop). Re-enrolling WITH HEAD TURNS (pan left/right/down during the 5s) made passive/any-angle recognition work great. schema.evolution_helper: src/lib/db.ts ensureColumn() + a one-shot UPDATE seeds: existing users get password='1234' WHERE password IS NULL on every db open (idempotent). user_role defaults to 'lobbyist' for new rows. NOTE: schema-add-on-running-dev-server requires full `next dev` restart — globalThis.__raamDb caches the connection.
schema.datetime_local_storage: system_messages.start_at and end_at store browser <input type="datetime-local"> wall-clock format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM); active query uses strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M', 'now', 'localtime') so comparisons stay in local time. whatsapp_messages.created_at uses SQLite CURRENT_TIMESTAMP which is UTC — the page's formatTime() helper treats them as UTC and renders in local time (he-IL locale).
schema.phone_match_strategy: WhatsApp inbound listener resolves resident by querying SELECT resident_id, number FROM phones, normalizing each via digitsOnly() and matching on the LAST 9 DIGITS. auth_system.model: Plain-text passwords stored in users.password. No encryption per user spec (lobby PC, localhost — anti-misclick, not anti-attacker).

known_bugs_in_runtime_NOT_FIXED: BUG 1 — logout() can hang. `await this.socket?.logout()` has no timeout. If the underlying WebSocket is degraded the protocol logout never acks and the await sits forever. The `finally` block is guarded by that await, so `fs.rm(AUTH_DIR)` never runs. Symptom: stale creds persist on disk; next connect hits the server with dead creds and the server returns `{reason:401, location:'cln'}` or `{reason:401, location:'atn'}` — Moti hit the 'atn' variant this session. Manual unblock: `rm -rf .baileys-auth/test-whatsapp`.

BUG 2 — listeners stay attached during/after wipe. Even when fs.rm runs, the socket's `creds.update` listener (saveCreds) and Baileys' internal key-store I/O can write fresh files into the just-wiped folder. Net effect: stale files can resurrect post-wipe.

FIX SKETCH (not applied yet, deferred A THIRD TIME): grab a local `socket` reference, immediately null `this.socket` + reset state, race `socket.logout()` against a 3s timeout, call `socket.end()`, remove all listeners (`socket.ev.removeAllListeners?.()` or equivalent), THEN await `fs.rm(AUTH_DIR, ...)`.
